Defendant Union Carbide'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-06-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows -- both Law and Motion judges having been peremptorily challenged, dDefendant Union Carbide's motion for summary judgment is ordered transferred to Dept. 206 for assignment. Parties to appear in that Dept. at 9:30 a.m. (302/REQ/RB-x) REPORTER: BELDON YEE CSR #2647

Defendant Union Carbide'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Assigned By Dept. 206 Per Ccp 170.6 As To Both Law & Motion Judges)
Off Calendar Oct-06-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

John R. Brydon, Esq., BRYDON HUGO & PARKER, 135 Main Street, 20th Floor, San Francisco, CA 94105, 415-808-0300 for moving party and defendant Union Carbide.

Carolin K. Shining, Esq., PAUL HANLEY & HARLEY, 1608 Fourth Street, Suite 300, Berkeley, CA 94710-1709, 510-559-9980

Defendant Union Carbide's Motion for Summary Judgment came on calendar this day in Department 501 before the Honorable James J. McBride by assignment from the Department of the Presiding Judge. The motion was argued and denied. The Court found that a triable issue of fact exists as to the special relation between Union Carbide and the end users. The Court overruled the objection to the Hamilton testimony. Plaintiff to prepare order. Reported by Joseph Vickstein, CSR #4780(501-JJM(ju))
